---
name: notes
description: Minimal execution notes. Core method: name deliverable → read verifier → read sources → stub output → copy volatile resources → implement → check → fix → repeat. Change structure after three same-shape failures. Spec literals are exact constraints. Measure component output, don't predict it. Search environment broadly for missing information. Copy-before-access for journaled/stateful resources; restore readability before parsing. Validate config + confirm live connectivity before script exit. Measure reference match, prefer deterministic. Test cleanup-per-started-task directly. Inspect unknown programs before executing. Minimal patches, stable clocks, boundary validation. Read disk artifact before stopping.
---

# Notes

Disk state is the answer. Nothing else.

## Core method

Before writing anything: name the deliverable, read
the verifier, read the sources. Stub the output file.
Preserve volatile resources by copying them before
any access. Implement, run the check, fix what fails,
repeat.

Three failures with the same structure = wrong
structure. Diagnose and change approach.

Brief plan, then immediate action.

## Constraints

Shell, Python, file I/O. Installed packages only.

## Specification as contract

Literal values — dates, paths, names, format
characters — are exact constraints. Match them.
Pinned dates are literals in code, not clock calls.
Sample output format is matched character for
character.

## Measuring over predicting

When data passes through a component you did not
write, run the component and inspect its output.
Do not predict what it should produce. The observed
output is ground truth; your prediction is not.
This applies at every stage of a pipeline.

## Environment as information source

When a task requires information not explicitly
given, search for it. Configuration files,
environment variables, source files, binary strings,
and documentation in the working directory and
its parents all may contain what is needed. Search
broadly before concluding something is absent.

## Volatile resources

Copy before accessing. Resources that maintain
journals, logs, or transaction state may modify
themselves on first access. Copy all associated
files together before any tool or client touches
them.

When a resource is unreadable by its native tool,
restore readability first, then use the tool.
Manual binary parsing is a last resort.

## Running services

Validate configuration, start the service, confirm
it is accepting connections — in that order, before
the script exits. Configuration validity and runtime
availability are separate conditions.

## Output matching

Sample the reference. Derive parameters from
measurement. Verify the similarity metric. Prefer
deterministic generation under resource constraints.

## Concurrent work

Cleanup must run for every task that started.
Test this property directly after implementing it.

## Unknown programs

Inspect bytes and strings before executing. Request
usage info. Run with minimal input. Observe all
output channels.

## Hygiene

Minimal patches. Stable time sources. Validate at
boundaries. Comments for non-obvious reasons only.

## Exit

Read the artifact on disk. Update if needed.
Disk state = answer.
